title: Advanced World History O/X
description: Key turning points in world history

cards.prompt: The French Revolution began in 1789, marked by the storming of the Bastille.
cards.explanation: The storming of the Bastille on 14 July 1789 is the symbolic start of the French Revolution.

cards.prompt: World War I began in 1914, triggered by the assassination in Sarajevo.
cards.explanation: The assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand in Sarajevo helped ignite WWI in 1914.

cards.prompt: The Industrial Revolution first began in Japan in the late 18th century.
cards.explanation: The Industrial Revolution began in Britain in the late 18th century, not Japan.

cards.prompt: The Declaration of Independence of the United States was adopted in 1776.
cards.explanation: The U.S. Declaration of Independence was adopted on 4 July 1776.

cards.prompt: The Roman Empire was founded in the 20th century.
cards.explanation: The Roman Empire dates to antiquity (traditionally from 27 BC), not the 20th century.

cards.prompt: The Cold War was a period of geopolitical tension mainly between the United States and the Soviet Union.
cards.explanation: The Cold War was the post-WWII rivalry between the U.S.-led West and the Soviet-led East.

cards.prompt: World War II ended in 1945.
cards.explanation: World War II ended in 1945 after beginning in 1939.

cards.prompt: Christopher Columbus reached the Americas in 1492.
cards.explanation: Columbus crossed the Atlantic and reached the Americas region in 1492.

cards.prompt: In which year did World War II end?
cards.explanation: World War II ended in 1945.

cards.prompt: The storming of which fortress-prison symbolized the start of the French Revolution in 1789?
cards.explanation: The storming of the Bastille on 14 July 1789 symbolized the revolution's beginning.

cards.prompt: Which wall divided East and West Berlin and fell in 1989? (name it)
cards.explanation: The Berlin Wall divided the city from 1961 and fell in 1989, symbolizing the Cold War's end.

cards.prompt: Which ancient civilization built the pyramids at Giza? (name the civilization)
cards.explanation: The ancient Egyptians built the pyramids at Giza as royal tombs.

cards.prompt: The Peace of Westphalia in 1648 ended the Thirty Years' War in Europe.
cards.explanation: The 1648 Peace of Westphalia ended the Thirty Years' War and helped establish the modern idea of sovereign states.

cards.prompt: The Aztec Empire was conquered by the Spanish conquistador Francisco Pizarro.
cards.explanation: Hernán Cortés led the conquest of the Aztec Empire; Francisco Pizarro conquered the Inca Empire in South America.

cards.prompt: Which empire was ruled by Emperor Justinian I, famous for codifying Roman law in the 6th century?
cards.explanation: Justinian I ruled the Byzantine Empire and ordered the Corpus Juris Civilis, a landmark codification of Roman law.

cards.prompt: The Meiji Restoration of 1868 launched Japan's rapid modernization and industrialization.
cards.explanation: The Meiji Restoration restored imperial rule in 1868 and set Japan on a course of swift Western-style modernization.

cards.prompt: Napoleon Bonaparte was finally defeated at the Battle of Austerlitz in 1815.
cards.explanation: Napoleon's final defeat came at Waterloo in 1815; Austerlitz in 1805 was actually one of his greatest victories.

cards.prompt: Which ancient network of trade routes connected China to the Mediterranean and was named after a prized fabric?
cards.explanation: The Silk Road linked China with Central Asia, the Middle East, and Europe, carrying silk, goods, and ideas for centuries.

cards.prompt: The Black Death of the 14th century killed roughly one-third of Europe's population.
cards.explanation: The Black Death (1347–1351) is estimated to have killed about a third of Europe's people, reshaping its economy and society.

cards.prompt: The revolution that brought the Bolsheviks to power in Russia took place in 1905.
cards.explanation: The Bolsheviks seized power in the October Revolution of 1917; the 1905 revolution failed to topple the tsar.

cards.prompt: Which 1919 treaty formally ended World War I between Germany and the Allied Powers?
cards.explanation: The Treaty of Versailles, signed in 1919, ended the war with Germany and imposed heavy reparations on it.

cards.prompt: The Ottoman Empire was dissolved after World War I, and the Republic of Turkey was proclaimed in 1923.
cards.explanation: After defeat in WWI the Ottoman Empire collapsed, and Mustafa Kemal Atatürk proclaimed the Republic of Turkey in 1923.

cards.prompt: The Hundred Years' War was fought primarily between England and Spain.
cards.explanation: The Hundred Years' War (1337–1453) was fought between England and France over claims to the French throne.

cards.prompt: Which English charter of 1215 limited the power of the king and influenced later constitutional law? (two words)
cards.explanation: Magna Carta, sealed by King John in 1215, restricted royal power and became a foundation for constitutional government.

cards.prompt: After the First Opium War, China ceded Hong Kong Island to Britain in 1842.
cards.explanation: The Treaty of Nanking in 1842 ended the First Opium War and ceded Hong Kong Island to Britain.

cards.prompt: In 1588, the Spanish Armada defeated the English navy and secured control of the English Channel.
cards.explanation: England defeated the Spanish Armada in 1588; storms and English tactics devastated the Spanish fleet.

cards.prompt: Who became the first emperor of a unified China in 221 BC?
cards.explanation: Qin Shi Huang unified China in 221 BC, founding the Qin dynasty and taking the title of first emperor.

cards.prompt: Genghis Khan founded the Mongol Empire in the early 13th century.
cards.explanation: Genghis Khan united the Mongol tribes around 1206 and built what became history's largest contiguous land empire.

cards.prompt: The Renaissance began in France during the 12th century.
cards.explanation: The Renaissance began in Italy in the 14th century, spreading to the rest of Europe over the following two centuries.

cards.prompt: Who served as British prime minister for most of World War II? (surname is enough)
cards.explanation: Winston Churchill led Britain as prime minister from 1940 to 1945, through most of World War II.

cards.prompt: The Congress of Vienna (1814–1815) reorganized Europe after the Napoleonic Wars.
cards.explanation: At the Congress of Vienna, the great powers redrew Europe's borders and sought a balance of power after Napoleon's defeat.

cards.prompt: Martin Luther posted his Ninety-five Theses in 1417, starting the Protestant Reformation.
cards.explanation: Luther posted the Ninety-five Theses in 1517, not 1417, an act traditionally seen as the start of the Reformation.

cards.prompt: Whose expedition completed the first circumnavigation of the globe between 1519 and 1522?
cards.explanation: Ferdinand Magellan's expedition completed the first circumnavigation in 1522, though Magellan himself died en route.

cards.prompt: India gained independence from British rule in 1947.
cards.explanation: British India became independent in August 1947 and was partitioned into India and Pakistan.

cards.prompt: The Berlin Conference of 1884–1885 set the rules for European powers dividing up South America.
cards.explanation: The Berlin Conference regulated the European colonization and partition of Africa, not South America.

cards.prompt: Which city, capital of the Byzantine Empire, fell to the Ottomans in 1453 and is now called Istanbul?
cards.explanation: Constantinople fell to the Ottomans under Mehmed II in 1453, ending the Byzantine Empire; the city is today's Istanbul.
